assertions.title: Deployed unknown routes return not-found behavior

steps.expected: Path remains /does-not-exist, not-found UI signal is present, and stale article content is absent
steps.observed: URL remained https://devblog-ten-brown.vercel.app/does-not-exist; title was Not Found | Portfolio; h1 was Not Found; visible not-found text signal detected; article selector count was 0.

steps.expected: Path remains /blog/does-not-exist, not-found UI signal is present, and stale article content is absent
steps.observed: URL remained https://devblog-ten-brown.vercel.app/blog/does-not-exist; title was Not Found | Portfolio; h1 was Not Found; visible not-found text signal detected; article selector count was 0.

steps.action: Collect HTTP status/header evidence for both unknown routes
steps.observed: HEAD checks returned HTTP 404 Not Found for both routes with Vercel 404 response headers (Content-Disposition: inline; filename=404.html).

evidence.consoleErrors: none (agent-browser errors --json returned [] after each route check)

frictions.description: PowerShell maps curl to Invoke-WebRequest alias, which does not support curl-style flags used for raw header capture.
frictions.resolution: Used curl.exe explicitly for HTTP header checks.

summary: Tested VAL-CROSS-005 on deployed Vercel URL: pass. Both unknown routes stayed on-path, rendered consistent not-found UI, showed no article content, and returned observable HTTP 404 semantics.
